title: cyananthus microphyllus subsp. microphyllus
intro: Cyananthus microphyllus subsp. microphyllus, also known as the dwarf delphinium or western blue larkspur, is a perennial plant species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds, offering several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and immune-boosting properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate various health issues.
